title: End-to-end multitask video retrieval with cross-attention
abstract: A method includes obtaining a video and a relational space-time query and identifying at least one type of the relational space-time query. The at least one identified type of the relational space-time query represents at least one of: an activity type, an object type, or a time type. The method also includes learning correlations among activities, objects, and time in the video using one or more cross-attention models. The method further includes obtaining one or more predictions generated using one or more outputs of the one or more cross-attention models based on the at least one identified type of the relational space-time query. In addition, the method includes generating a response to the relational space-time query based on the one or more predictions.
claims: Claims ( 15 ) A method comprising: obtaining a video and a relational space-time query; identifying at least one type of the relational space-time query, the at least one identified type of the relational space-time query representing at least one of: an activity type, an object type, or a time type; learning correlations among activities, objects, and time in the video using one or more cross-attention models; obtaining one or more predictions generated using one or more outputs of the one or more cross-attention models based on the at least one identified type of the relational space-time query; and generating a response to the relational space-time query based on the one or more predictions. The method of Claim 1, further comprising: splitting the video into multiple segments; wherein the one or more predictions are generated using the one or more outputs of the one or more cross-attention models for each of the multiple segments. The method of Claim 2, wherein: the at least one type of the relational space-time query includes the activity type; and the method further comprises combining the predictions for the multiple segments into a vector in order to generate an activity prediction for the video. The method of Claim 2, wherein: the at least one type of the relational space-time query includes the object type; splitting the video into the multiple segments comprises splitting the video into separate image frames; and the method further comprises generating a list including the predictions for the multiple segments in order to generate an object prediction for the video. The method of Claim 2, wherein: the at least one type of the relational space-time query includes the time type; each prediction is associated with a start time and an end time; and the method further comprises stitching at least two of the predictions together based on at least some of their start and end times in order to generate a time prediction for the video. The method of Claim 1, wherein learning the correlations among the activities, objects, and time in the video comprises: generating embeddings of at least some image frames in the video and embeddings of at least one of an activity or an object identified by the relational space-time query; identifying one or more tokens for each of the at least some image frames in the video and for at least one of the activity or the object identified by the relational space-time query based on the embeddings; performing at least one of activity cross-attention, object cross-attention, or time cross-attention based on the tokens; and processing results of at least one of the activity cross-attention, object cross-attention, or time cross-attention in order to generate at least one of an activity prediction, an object prediction, or a time prediction. The method of Claim 1, wherein the one or more cross-attention models represent a single cross-attention model. An electronic device comprising: at least one processing device configured to: obtain a video and a relational space-time query; identify at least one type of the relational space-time query, the at least one identified type of the relational space-time query representing at least one of: an activity type, an object type, or a time type; learn correlations among activities, objects, and time in the video using one or more cross-attention models; obtain one or more predictions generated using one or more outputs of the one or more cross-attention models based on the at least one identified type of the relational space-time query; and generate a response to the relational space-time query based on the one or more predictions. The electronic device of Claim 8, wherein the at least one processing device is further configured to: split the video into multiple segments; and generate the one or more predictions using the one or more outputs of the one or more cross-attention models for each of the multiple segments. The electronic device of Claim 9, wherein: the at least one type of the relational space-time query includes the activity type; and the at least one processing device is further configured to combine the predictions for the multiple segments into a vector in order to generate an activity prediction for the video. The electronic device of Claim 9, wherein: the at least one type of the relational space-time query includes the object type; to split the video into the multiple segments, the at least one processing device is configured to split the video into separate image frames; and the at least one processing device is further configured to generate a list including the predictions for the multiple segments in order to generate an object prediction for the video. The electronic device of Claim 9, wherein: the at least one type of the relational space-time query includes the time type; each prediction is associated with a start time and an end time; and the at least one processing device is further configured to stitch at least two of the predictions together based on at least some of their start and end times in order to generate a time prediction for the video. The electronic device of Claim 8, wherein, to learn the correlations among the activities, objects, and time in the video, the at least one processing device is configured to: generate embeddings of at least some image frames in the video and embeddings of at least one of an activity or an object identified by the relational space-time query; identify one or more tokens for each of the at least some image frames in the video and for at least one of the activity or the object identified by the relational space-time query based on the embeddings; perform at least one of activity cross-attention, object cross-attention, or time cross-attention based on the tokens; and process results of at least one of the activity cross-attention, object cross-attention, or time cross-attention in order to generate at least one of an activity prediction, an object prediction, or a time prediction. The electronic device of Claim 8, wherein the one or more cross-attention models represent a single cross-attention model. A non-transitory machine readable medium containing instructions that when executed cause at least one processor of an electronic device to: obtain a video and a relational space-time query; identify at least one type of the relational space-time query, the at least one identified type of the relational space-time query representing at least one of: an activity type, an object type, or a time type; learn correlations among activities, objects, and time in the video using one or more cross-attention models; obtain one or more predictions generated using one or more outputs of the one or more cross-attention models based on the at least one identified type of the relational space-time query; and generate a response to the relational space-time query based on the one or more predictions.
